title: Lemma B: Strong existence + pathwise uniqueness when R=(I+G)^{-1} is a nonsingular M-matrix (Lipschitz Skorokhod map)

comments.author: human
comments.body:
**Claim:** If $R=(I+G)^{-1}$ satisfies the Harrison-Reiman condition (after diagonal normalization $R=I-Q$ with $Q\ge0$ entrywise and $\rho(Q)<1$; equivalently $R$ is a nonsingular M-matrix), then the Skorokhod reflection map $\Phi:X^\ast\mapsto W^\ast$ is single-valued and Lipschitz in the sup norm on compacts. Hence for the BM driver $X^\ast$ there is a **pathwise unique** $(W^\ast,Y^\ast)$ satisfying (i)-(iv).

**What must be written out (do not cite as 'standard'):**
1. The fixed-point / Picard construction of $Y^\ast$ as the limit of the oscillation map.
2. The contraction / oscillation estimate proving uniqueness and Lipschitz dependence.
3. Verification that the limit satisfies all of (i)-(iv) including $\int_0^\infty W^\ast_j\,dY^\ast_j=0$.

Reference: Harrison & Reiman, Ann. Probab. 9 (1981) 302-308.
